description: Migrate all file-based simulation storage to PostgreSQL (structured state) and Qdrant (embedding vectors). Zero JSON file I/O for simulation data after completion.

userStories.title: Add Qdrant service to Docker and install client library
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Qdrant running in Docker with the client library installed so the rest of the migration can use it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `qdrant/qdrant` service to `docker-compose.yml` with persistent named volume `qdrant-data`
Qdrant port 6333 exposed with a Docker healthcheck
Add `qdrant-client` to `backend_server/requirements.txt`
Add `qdrant-client` to `frontend_server/requirements.txt`
`docker compose up` starts Qdrant alongside PostgreSQL without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create qdrant_utils.py module with core embedding operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable Qdrant utility module so all embedding operations go through a single interface.
acceptanceCriteria: File created at `backend_server/utils/qdrant_utils.py`
`store_embedding(persona_id, simulation_id, embedding_key, vector)` upserts a point into `persona_embeddings` collection
`get_embedding(persona_id, embedding_key)` retrieves a single vector by payload filter
`search_similar(persona_id, query_vector, top_k=20)` returns top-k nearest embedding_keys using cosine ANN search
`delete_persona_embeddings(persona_id)` removes all points for a persona
`copy_persona_embeddings(src_persona_id, dst_persona_id, dst_simulation_id)` deep-copies all vectors for fork operations
`persona_embeddings` collection auto-created on first use with dimension from `EMBEDDING_DIMENSION` env var (default 1536) and cosine distance
Qdrant client is a module-level singleton (not recreated per call)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Simulation and Persona Django ORM models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Django ORM models for the core simulation and persona entities so simulation metadata and agent identities can be stored in PostgreSQL.
acceptanceCriteria: `Simulation` model has fields: id, name (unique), description, status (default 'pending'), fork_sim_code, start_date, curr_time, sec_per_step, maze_name, step, config (JSONB), created_at, updated_at
`Persona` model has fields: id, simulation (FK), name, first_name, last_name, age, innate, learned, currently, lifestyle, living_area, daily_plan_req, status (default 'active')
`Persona` has UNIQUE constraint on (simulation_id, name)
Migration generated and applies cleanly against PostgreSQL
Both models registered in Django admin with list_display and search_fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create PersonaScratch Django ORM model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a PersonaScratch model to store all 40+ mutable short-term memory fields for each persona.
acceptanceCriteria: `PersonaScratch` model has one-to-one FK to `Persona`
Model includes all perception fields: vision_r, att_bandwidth, retention
Model includes all temporal fields: curr_time (TIMESTAMPTZ), curr_tile (JSONB), concept_forget, daily_reflection_time, daily_reflection_size

Model includes all scoring fields: overlap_reflect_th, kw_strg_event_reflect_th, kw_strg_thought_reflect_th, recency_w, relevance_w, importance_w, recency_decay, importance_trigger_max, importance_trigger_curr, importance_ele_n, thought_count
Model includes all schedule fields: daily_req (JSONB), f_daily_schedule (JSONB), f_daily_schedule_hourly_org (JSONB)
Model includes all action fields: act_address, act_start_time, act_duration, act_description, act_pronunciatio, act_event (JSONB), act_obj_description, act_obj_pronunciatio, act_obj_event (JSONB)
Model includes all chat fields: chatting_with, chat (JSONB), chatting_with_buffer (JSONB), chatting_end_time, act_path_set, planned_path (JSONB)
Migration generated and applies cleanly
Registered in Django admin
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create EnvironmentState and MovementRecord Django ORM models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need models for per-step environment and movement data so the simulation game loop can read and write through the database.
acceptanceCriteria: `EnvironmentState` model has fields: id, simulation (FK), step (INT), agent_positions (JSONB NOT NULL)
`EnvironmentState` has UNIQUE constraint on (simulation_id, step)
`MovementRecord` model has fields: id, simulation (FK), step (INT), sim_curr_time, persona_movements (JSONB NOT NULL)
`MovementRecord` has UNIQUE constraint on (simulation_id, step)
Both models indexed on (simulation_id, step) for fast game-loop lookups
Migration generated and applies cleanly
Both models registered in Django admin
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create ConceptNode and KeywordStrength Django ORM models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need models for associative memory nodes and keyword strength index so long-term agent memory can be stored in PostgreSQL.
acceptanceCriteria: `ConceptNode` model has fields: id, persona (FK), node_id, node_count, type_count, node_type (event/thought/chat), depth, created (TIMESTAMPTZ), expiration, last_accessed, subject, predicate, object, description, embedding_key, poignancy, keywords (JSONB), filling (JSONB)
`ConceptNode` has UNIQUE constraint on (persona_id, node_id)
`ConceptNode` has indexes on (persona_id, node_type), (persona_id, subject), (persona_id, created DESC)
`KeywordStrength` model has fields: id, persona (FK), keyword, strength_type ('event' or 'thought'), strength (INT default 0)
`KeywordStrength` has UNIQUE constraint on (persona_id, keyword, strength_type)
Migration generated and applies cleanly
Both models registered in Django admin with search_fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create SpatialMemory, RuntimeState, Demo, DemoMovement, and Conversation models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining Django ORM models for spatial memory, IPC state, demo replay data, and conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: `SpatialMemory` model has one-to-one FK to Persona and a `tree` JSONB field (default '{}')
`RuntimeState` model has fields: id, key (VARCHAR UNIQUE), value (JSONB default '{}'), updated_at
`Demo` model has fields: id, name (UNIQUE), fork_sim_code, start_date, curr_time, sec_per_step, maze_name, persona_names (JSONB), step, total_steps, created_at
`DemoMovement` model has fields: id, demo (FK), step (INT), agent_movements (JSONB NOT NULL), UNIQUE(demo_id, step)
`Conversation` model has fields: id, simulation (FK), started_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), transcript (JSONB default '[]')
`ConversationParticipant` model has fields: id, conversation (FK), persona (FK), UNIQUE(conversation_id, persona_id)
Migration generated and applies cleanly
All models registered in Django admin
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Replace temp-file IPC with RuntimeState DB table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need temp-file IPC replaced with the RuntimeState table so the backend and frontend communicate simulation state through the database instead of temp files.
acceptanceCriteria: Helper functions `set_runtime_state(key, value)` and `get_runtime_state(key, default=None)` created in `backend_server/utils/db_utils.py`
`reverie.py` reads/writes `curr_sim_code` and `curr_step` via RuntimeState instead of temp files
`reverie.py` reads `path_tester_env` and writes `path_tester_out` via RuntimeState
`frontend_server/translator/views.py` `path_tester_update` writes to RuntimeState instead of `temp_storage/path_tester_env.json`
`fs_temp_storage` constant removed from `constant.py`
Zero remaining references to `temp_storage/` in Python source files
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor Scratch class to load/save from persona_scratch table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Scratch class to persist to the persona_scratch table so short-term persona state survives without JSON files.
acceptanceCriteria: `Scratch.__init__` accepts a `persona_id` int argument for DB mode; legacy file-path argument retained for import command only
`Scratch.load_from_db(persona_id)` class method loads all fields from the PersonaScratch row
`Scratch.save()` writes all fields to PersonaScratch via Django ORM and updates identity fields on the parent Persona row
All JSONB fields (daily_req, f_daily_schedule, curr_tile, act_event, planned_path, etc.) store and load as Python lists/dicts correctly
Datetime fields serialize/deserialize correctly between Python datetime objects and TIMESTAMPTZ columns
`save()` then `load_from_db()` produces identical state for all 40+ fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor AssociativeMemory to load/save from PostgreSQL and Qdrant
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the associative memory class to persist concept nodes in PostgreSQL and embedding vectors in Qdrant, replacing embeddings.json, nodes.json, and kw_strength.json.
acceptanceCriteria: `AssociativeMemory.__init__` accepts `persona_id` and loads all ConceptNode rows from DB
`AssociativeMemory.__init__` loads keyword strengths from KeywordStrength table
`AssociativeMemory.__init__` loads embedding vectors from Qdrant (not from embeddings.json)
`add_event`, `add_thought`, `add_chat` write the new ConceptNode row to DB immediately on creation
`add_event`, `add_thought`, `add_chat` store the embedding vector in Qdrant via `qdrant_utils.store_embedding()`
`add_event` and `add_thought` update KeywordStrength rows using Django `F()` expressions for atomic increment
In-memory caches (id_to_node, seq_event, kw_to_event, etc.) maintained for fast runtime access
`retrieve_relevant_events`, `retrieve_relevant_thoughts`, `get_last_chat` return correct results with DB-loaded data
Similarity retrieval uses `qdrant_utils.search_similar()` then looks up matched keys from in-memory cache
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor SpatialMemory to load/save from spatial_memories table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the spatial memory tree to persist in the spatial_memories table instead of spatial_memory.json.
acceptanceCriteria: `MemoryTree.__init__` accepts `persona_id` and loads `tree` JSONB from SpatialMemory row (creates row if missing)
`MemoryTree.save()` writes `tree` JSONB to SpatialMemory via Django ORM
`get_str_accessible_sectors`, `get_str_accessible_sector_arenas`, `get_str_accessible_arena_game_objects` return identical results with DB-loaded data
Tree structure round-trips correctly: `save()` then `load` produces identical nested dict
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor Persona class to initialize all memory structures from database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Persona class to initialize scratch, associative memory, and spatial memory from the database with no file path construction.
acceptanceCriteria: `Persona.__init__` accepts a `persona_id` int (DB mode) or file path string (legacy mode for import only)
`Persona.__init__` in DB mode initializes `scratch` via `Scratch.load_from_db(persona_id)`
`Persona.__init__` in DB mode initializes `a_mem` via `AssociativeMemory(persona_id)`
`Persona.__init__` in DB mode initializes `s_mem` via `MemoryTree(persona_id)`
`Persona.save()` calls DB-backed save on all three memory structures
No `bootstrap_memory/scratch.json` or similar file path construction in DB mode
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor reverie.py — simulation init and persona loading from PostgreSQL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the simulation engine to load all startup state (simulation metadata, personas, initial environment) from PostgreSQL instead of JSON files.
acceptanceCriteria: `ReverieServer.__init__` loads simulation metadata from Simulation model instead of `meta.json`
`ReverieServer.__init__` loads initial environment state from EnvironmentState model instead of `environment/{step}.json`
`ReverieServer.__init__` initializes all Persona objects via `Persona(persona_id=<id>)` DB mode
No `json.load(open(...))` calls for meta or environment data in `__init__`
`fs_storage` constant no longer referenced in `reverie.py`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor reverie.py — simulation save and per-step execution to PostgreSQL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the simulation save and step execution loop to write all state to PostgreSQL so no JSON files are written during a running simulation.
acceptanceCriteria: `ReverieServer.save()` writes Simulation metadata, Persona, PersonaScratch, and EnvironmentState rows via Django ORM instead of JSON files
`ReverieServer.start_server()` writes MovementRecord rows instead of `movement/{step}.json`
`ReverieServer.start_server()` updates `Simulation.step` and `Simulation.curr_time` in DB after each step
`ReverieServer.start_server()` updates RuntimeState with `curr_sim_code` and `curr_step`
Each simulation step wrapped in `django.db.transaction.atomic()`
`ReverieServer.start_path_tester_server()` reads/writes via RuntimeState only
Zero `json.dump` calls for simulation data in `reverie.py`
Simulation can be started, run for 10+ steps, paused, and resumed entirely from DB state
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor api_views.py — simulation list, create, and fork endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the simulation list, create, and fork API endpoints to use PostgreSQL so filesystem scanning and shutil.copytree are eliminated.
acceptanceCriteria: `simulations_list` GET queries `Simulation.objects.all()` instead of `os.listdir(STORAGE_DIR)`
`simulations_list` POST creates a Simulation row via Django ORM with no `meta.json` file write
Fork operation creates new Simulation row and deep-copies Persona, PersonaScratch, ConceptNode, KeywordStrength, and SpatialMemory rows with new PKs
Fork operation calls `qdrant_utils.copy_persona_embeddings()` for each forked persona
No `shutil.copytree` call for fork in `api_views.py`
API response format for list and create is identical to pre-migration output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor api_views.py — simulation detail, agents, state, and demo endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining REST API endpoints to read from PostgreSQL instead of scanning the filesystem.
acceptanceCriteria: `simulation_detail` queries Simulation model by name
`simulation_state` queries EnvironmentState for the latest step of the simulation
`simulation_agents` queries Persona + PersonaScratch joined with the latest EnvironmentState
`simulation_agent_detail` queries Persona + PersonaScratch by persona name
`demos_list` queries `Demo.objects.all()` instead of `os.listdir(COMPRESSED_STORAGE_DIR)`
`demo_step` queries DemoMovement by step number instead of loading `master_movement.json`
All `STORAGE_DIR` and `COMPRESSED_STORAGE_DIR` path constants removed from `api_views.py`
All `os.path`, `os.listdir`, `json.load`, `shutil` calls removed from `api_views.py`
API response format for all endpoints is identical to pre-migration output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor views.py game loop endpoints to use PostgreSQL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Phaser game loop endpoints to read and write simulation state from PostgreSQL so no JSON files are written during game ticks.
acceptanceCriteria: `process_environment` writes agent positions to EnvironmentState model instead of `storage/{sim}/environment/{step}.json`
`update_environment` reads movement data from MovementRecord model instead of `storage/{sim}/movement/{step}.json`
`path_tester_update` writes to RuntimeState table instead of `temp_storage/path_tester_env.json`
Response format for all three endpoints is identical to pre-migration output
File I/O imports (`json`, `os` for storage paths) removed from `views.py`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build import_simulation management command for all existing simulations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a management command that migrates all existing file-based simulations into PostgreSQL and Qdrant so historical data is preserved on first deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: Command `python manage.py import_simulation <sim_code>` imports a single simulation
Command `python manage.py import_simulation --all` imports all simulations in `storage/`
Imports `reverie/meta.json` → Simulation row
Imports all `environment/*.json` → EnvironmentState rows via `bulk_create(batch_size=500)`
Imports all `movement/*.json` → MovementRecord rows via `bulk_create(batch_size=500)`
Imports each persona's `scratch.json` → Persona + PersonaScratch rows
Imports each persona's `spatial_memory.json` → SpatialMemory row
Imports each persona's `nodes.json` → ConceptNode rows via `bulk_create(batch_size=500)`
Imports each persona's `kw_strength.json` → KeywordStrength rows via `bulk_create`
Imports each persona's `embeddings.json` → Qdrant `persona_embeddings` collection via batch upsert
Command is idempotent: re-running does not duplicate data (uses update_or_create or update_conflicts=True)
Prints progress: 'Imported sim X: N personas, M concept nodes, K embeddings, J environment steps'
Missing or corrupted files handled with warnings without aborting the entire import
Each simulation import wrapped in `transaction.atomic()`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build import_demo and export_simulation management commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an import command for compressed demo data and an export command for database-to-file backup.
acceptanceCriteria: Command `python manage.py import_demo <demo_code>` imports a single demo from `compressed_storage/`
Command `python manage.py import_demo --all` imports all demos in `compressed_storage/`
Demo import: reads `meta.json` → Demo row; reads `master_movement.json` → individual DemoMovement rows via bulk_create
Demo import is idempotent
Prints progress: 'Imported demo X: N movement steps'
Command `python manage.py export_simulation <sim_name> --output <dir>` exports to the original file layout (reverie/meta.json, environment/*.json, movement/*.json, personas/*/bootstrap_memory/*)
Export includes embeddings from Qdrant → `embeddings.json` per persona
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor compress_sim_storage.py and remove all file storage infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the compression utility refactored to use the database and all remaining file storage code removed so there is a single source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: `compress_sim_storage.py` `compress()` queries Simulation, MovementRecord, and Persona from DB instead of scanning `storage/`
`compress_sim_storage.py` writes output to Demo + DemoMovement tables instead of `compressed_storage/` files
All file path references removed from `compress_sim_storage.py`
`constant.py` `fs_storage` and `fs_temp_storage` variables removed
Docker `simulation-storage` and `simulation-temp` volumes removed from `docker-compose.yml` (if present)
Dockerfile steps that `mkdir storage temp_storage` removed
Zero remaining `open()` calls for simulation data in `backend_server/` and `frontend_server/translator/` (verified by grep)
`storage/` and `temp_storage/` directories no longer required at runtime
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write integration tests for DB-backed simulation lifecycle
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests verifying the full simulation lifecycle works through PostgreSQL and Qdrant.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: create simulation via API → Simulation row exists in DB
Test: fork simulation → all Persona, PersonaScratch, ConceptNode, KeywordStrength, SpatialMemory rows copied with new PKs; Qdrant embeddings copied
Test: `process_environment` POST → EnvironmentState row created with correct agent_positions
Test: `update_environment` GET → reads from MovementRecord and returns correct persona_movements
Test: save PersonaScratch → all 40+ fields round-trip through DB correctly
Test: save/load AssociativeMemory → all concept nodes present in DB; embeddings retrievable from Qdrant
Test: save/load SpatialMemory → tree structure round-trips through JSONB
Test: `import_simulation` for base_the_ville_isabella_maria_klaus → all data present in DB and Qdrant
Test: `export_simulation` → file output matches original file format
Test: Qdrant `search_similar` → returns results ordered by cosine similarity
All tests run against PostgreSQL 16 (not SQLite)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: End-to-end validation: run full simulation from DB-backed storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify a complete simulation run works correctly with DB-backed storage from import through gameplay to demo compression.
acceptanceCriteria: Import `base_the_ville_isabella_maria_klaus` via `import_simulation` without errors
Start simulation via `reverie.py` — runs for at least 10 steps without errors or exceptions
EnvironmentState rows accumulate with correct agent positions after each step
MovementRecord rows accumulate with correct movement data after each step
ConceptNode rows accumulate in DB as simulation runs
Embeddings are stored and searchable in Qdrant after simulation steps
Simulation list API returns the simulation with correct metadata
Run `compress_sim_storage.py` → Demo and DemoMovement rows created in DB
Demo step API endpoint returns correct movement data from DemoMovement rows
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
